revision.comment: Text replacement - "|平将門}}" to "|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3年}}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3年}}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=draft}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=draft}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌|12=draft}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.

== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiyoda|ja|千代田区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda|qq=}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Dōkan|qq=}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

revision.comment: 64 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:築土神社 up to rev 102215482
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 築土神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[東京都]][[千代田区]]九段北1-14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|46|N|139|45|0|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ニニギ|天津彦火邇々杵尊]]、[[平将門]]{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[940年]]{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |本殿 = |別名 = 築土明神 |例祭 = 9月15日{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|label=築土神社|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|41|46|N|139|45|0|E}}}} }} '''築土神社'''（つくどじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[千代田区]][[九段]]にある[[神社]]である。通称、'''築土明神'''。 創建時の祭神・[[平将門]]に因み、武勇長久の神社として親しまれ、千代田区[[北の丸公園]]にある[[日本武道館]]の[[氏神]]でもある。 毎年正月に授与される'''[[勝守]]'''（かちまもり）は有名。 == 祭神 == 現在は[[ニニギ|天津彦火邇々杵尊]]（あまつひこほのににぎのみこと）を主祭神とし、平将門、[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。 [[江戸時代]]の文献によると、当社内には平将門の首（頭蓋骨や髪の毛）そのものが安置されていたといわれ、数ある将門ゆかりの社寺の中で、将門信仰の象徴的神社となっていた。明治に[[教部省]]の指示により将門は相殿に格下げされ、現在は天津彦火邇々杵尊が当社の主祭神となっている。 戦災で当社が焼失するまで、将門の首を納めたという首桶{{efn|御神体の「首桶」なるものは、『[[東京市史稿]]』市街篇第2巻186頁に写真が残されている。蓋上面に「江戸／上平川」、側面に「江戸郷／上平河村」と墨書されており、何らかの神宝を納めるために作られた中世の曲物(まげもの)であったようである。}}、将門の肖像画（束帯姿）、木造の束帯坐像等が社宝として伝わっていた。昭和20年4月、戦災により社殿とともにそれらは焼失し、現在は一部の写真が残るのみである。 [[拝殿]]の装飾や[[絵馬]]などには、[[巴紋]]のほか平将門に因んだ[[繋ぎ馬]]（つなぎうま）の紋が使用されている。これは神社境内にある[[天水桶]]（[[文政]]元年）の彫刻を模したもので、築土神社の[[登録商標]]である（平成23年現在）。 == 歴史 == *[[天慶]]3年{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=517}}（[[940年]]）6月、[[江戸]]の津久戸村(上平川村{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、現：[[千代田区]][[大手町 (千代田区)|大手町]]一丁目[[将門塚]]付近）に平将門の首を祀り、塚を築いたことから「'''津久戸明神'''」として{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=517}}創建されたという。 *[[室町時代]]{{efn|『江戸名所図会』では[[天正]]7年（1580年）{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}。}}に[[太田道灌]]により田安郷（現：千代田区九段坂上）へ移転させられて以降は「'''田安明神'''」とも呼ばれ{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]、[[神田明神]]とともに江戸三社の一つにも数えられることもあった（江戸三社のうち、日枝神社、神田明神以外は固定していない）。 *[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年([[1616年]])に[[江戸城]]の拡張工事により[[筑土八幡神社]]隣接地（現：[[新宿区]][[筑土八幡町]]）へ移転し{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、「'''築土明神'''」と呼ばれた。 *[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）天津彦火邇々杵尊を主祭神として「'''築土神社'''」へ改称する。 *300年以上の間、筑土八幡神社と並んで鎮座していたが、[[1945年]]の[[東京大空襲]]によって全焼し、[[1954年]]、現在の九段中坂の途中にある世継稲荷境内地へ移転した。 *[[平成]]6年（[[1994年]]）、境内地にオフィスビルを建設するとともに、鉄筋コンクリート造の社殿を新築。 *[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）の築土祭では、[[安政]]6年（1859）の[[神田明神]]以来、実に147年振りに[[神輿]]渡御行列の[[江戸城]]入りを果たしている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *9月15日の例大祭に至る一週間の行事を「築土祭」と呼び、節目の年には神輿渡御などが行われる。 == 氏子地域 == * [[千代田区]][[九段北]]一･二丁目 * 千代田区[[九段南]]一･二丁目 * 千代田区[[飯田橋]] * 千代田区[[富士見 (千代田区)|富士見]] * 千代田区[[北の丸公園]] * 千代田区[[一ツ橋]]一丁目 * [[新宿区]][[市谷船河原町]] - 当地に摂社があるため、[[飛地]]状態で氏子地域となっている。{{Efn|築土神社は、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）まで、氏子地域から離れた新宿区[[筑土八幡町]]にあり、[[筑土八幡神社]]と並んで鎮座していた。この一帯は[[筑土八幡神社]]の氏子地域である。[[市谷船河原町]]の外堀通りから[[アンスティチュ・フランセ東京]](旧東京日仏学院)への入口にある摂社は、古代の「さねかずら」という美女にまつわる「掘兼の井」の故地であるとする伝承がある。『[[江戸名所記]]』所収「掘兼の井」の記述はここをさすと見られる。当町にはかつて氏子総代をつとめた旧家があり、平安時代に京都でさらされていた[[平将門]]の首級を密かに奪い取って持ち帰った者の子孫であったと言い伝えられていたという。}} == 文化財 == ;狛犬（筑土神社）: 千代田区指定文化財有形民俗文化財{{sfn|千代田区}} ;力石（筑土神社）: 千代田区指定文化財有形民俗文化財{{sfn|千代田区}} == 所在地・交通 == 東京都千代田区九段北1-14-21 *[[九段下駅]]下車すぐ == ギャラリー == Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|中坂に面した参道 Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|[[安永 (元号)|安永]]九年（[[1780年]]）銘の[[狛犬]]（阿形） Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|世継稲荷 Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|靖国通り・北の丸スクエア方面から本殿を望む == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=[[江戸名所図会]]|id={{NDLJP|1174144/263}}|publisher=有朋堂書店|editor=斎藤長秋|volume=2|series=有朋堂文庫|year=1927|chapter=巻之四 天権之部 津久戸明神社/筑土八幡宮|pages=517-521|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tsukudo Shrine}} *[[平将門]] *[[筑土八幡神社]]…戦前まで隣に並んでいた[[八幡宮]]。現在でも筑土八幡町にある。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ 築土神社/平将門を祀る江戸の古社] * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/2006/ 築土祭～よみがえる江戸の情熱（はな）～] * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://edo-chiyoda.jp/chiyoda-yukei_minzoku.html|title=有形民俗文化財|website=千代田区の文化財|publisher= 千代田区|accessdate=2020-6-15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/|title=築土神社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-6-15|ref=harv}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つくとしんしや}} [[Category:千代田区の神社]] revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[築土神社]] to [[Tsukudo Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 築土神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[東京都]][[千代田区]]九段北1-14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|46|N|139|45|0|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ニニギ|天津彦火邇々杵尊]]、[[平将門]]{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[940年]]{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |本殿 = |別名 = 築土明神 |例祭 = 9月15日{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|label=築土神社|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|41|46|N|139|45|0|E}}}} }} '''築土神社'''（つくどじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[千代田区]][[九段]]にある[[神社]]である。通称、'''築土明神'''。 創建時の祭神・[[平将門]]に因み、武勇長久の神社として親しまれ、千代田区[[北の丸公園]]にある[[日本武道館]]の[[氏神]]でもある。 毎年正月に授与される'''[[勝守]]'''（かちまもり）は有名。 == 祭神 == 現在は[[ニニギ|天津彦火邇々杵尊]]（あまつひこほのににぎのみこと）を主祭神とし、平将門、[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。 [[江戸時代]]の文献によると、当社内には平将門の首（頭蓋骨や髪の毛）そのものが安置されていたといわれ、数ある将門ゆかりの社寺の中で、将門信仰の象徴的神社となっていた。明治に[[教部省]]の指示により将門は相殿に格下げされ、現在は天津彦火邇々杵尊が当社の主祭神となっている。 戦災で当社が焼失するまで、将門の首を納めたという首桶{{efn|御神体の「首桶」なるものは、『[[東京市史稿]]』市街篇第2巻186頁に写真が残されている。蓋上面に「江戸／上平川」、側面に「江戸郷／上平河村」と墨書されており、何らかの神宝を納めるために作られた中世の曲物(まげもの)であったようである。}}、将門の肖像画（束帯姿）、木造の束帯坐像等が社宝として伝わっていた。昭和20年4月、戦災により社殿とともにそれらは焼失し、現在は一部の写真が残るのみである。 [[拝殿]]の装飾や[[絵馬]]などには、[[巴紋]]のほか平将門に因んだ[[繋ぎ馬]]（つなぎうま）の紋が使用されている。これは神社境内にある[[天水桶]]（[[文政]]元年）の彫刻を模したもので、築土神社の[[登録商標]]である（平成23年現在）。 == 歴史 == *[[天慶]]3年{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=517}}（[[940年]]）6月、[[江戸]]の津久戸村(上平川村{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、現：[[千代田区]][[大手町 (千代田区)|大手町]]一丁目[[将門塚]]付近）に平将門の首を祀り、塚を築いたことから「'''津久戸明神'''」として{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=517}}創建されたという。 *[[室町時代]]{{efn|『江戸名所図会』では[[天正]]7年（1580年）{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}。}}に[[太田道灌]]により田安郷（現：千代田区九段坂上）へ移転させられて以降は「'''田安明神'''」とも呼ばれ{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]、[[神田明神]]とともに江戸三社の一つにも数えられることもあった（江戸三社のうち、日枝神社、神田明神以外は固定していない）。 *[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年([[1616年]])に[[江戸城]]の拡張工事により[[筑土八幡神社]]隣接地（現：[[新宿区]][[筑土八幡町]]）へ移転し{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=520}}、「'''築土明神'''」と呼ばれた。 *[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）天津彦火邇々杵尊を主祭神として「'''築土神社'''」へ改称する。 *300年以上の間、筑土八幡神社と並んで鎮座していたが、[[1945年]]の[[東京大空襲]]によって全焼し、[[1954年]]、現在の九段中坂の途中にある世継稲荷境内地へ移転した。 *[[平成]]6年（[[1994年]]）、境内地にオフィスビルを建設するとともに、鉄筋コンクリート造の社殿を新築。 *[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）の築土祭では、[[安政]]6年（1859）の[[神田明神]]以来、実に147年振りに[[神輿]]渡御行列の[[江戸城]]入りを果たしている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *9月15日の例大祭に至る一週間の行事を「築土祭」と呼び、節目の年には神輿渡御などが行われる。 == 氏子地域 == * [[千代田区]][[九段北]]一･二丁目 * 千代田区[[九段南]]一･二丁目 * 千代田区[[飯田橋]] * 千代田区[[富士見 (千代田区)|富士見]] * 千代田区[[北の丸公園]] * 千代田区[[一ツ橋]]一丁目 * [[新宿区]][[市谷船河原町]] - 当地に摂社があるため、[[飛地]]状態で氏子地域となっている。{{Efn|築土神社は、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）まで、氏子地域から離れた新宿区[[筑土八幡町]]にあり、[[筑土八幡神社]]と並んで鎮座していた。この一帯は[[筑土八幡神社]]の氏子地域である。[[市谷船河原町]]の外堀通りから[[アンスティチュ・フランセ東京]](旧東京日仏学院)への入口にある摂社は、古代の「さねかずら」という美女にまつわる「掘兼の井」の故地であるとする伝承がある。『[[江戸名所記]]』所収「掘兼の井」の記述はここをさすと見られる。当町にはかつて氏子総代をつとめた旧家があり、平安時代に京都でさらされていた[[平将門]]の首級を密かに奪い取って持ち帰った者の子孫であったと言い伝えられていたという。}} == 文化財 == ;狛犬（筑土神社）: 千代田区指定文化財有形民俗文化財{{sfn|千代田区}} ;力石（筑土神社）: 千代田区指定文化財有形民俗文化財{{sfn|千代田区}} == 所在地・交通 == 東京都千代田区九段北1-14-21 *[[九段下駅]]下車すぐ == ギャラリー == Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|中坂に面した参道 Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|[[安永 (元号)|安永]]九年（[[1780年]]）銘の[[狛犬]]（阿形） Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|世継稲荷 Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|靖国通り・北の丸スクエア方面から本殿を望む == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=[[江戸名所図会]]|id={{NDLJP|1174144/263}}|publisher=有朋堂書店|editor=斎藤長秋|volume=2|series=有朋堂文庫|year=1927|chapter=巻之四 天権之部 津久戸明神社/筑土八幡宮|pages=517-521|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tsukudo Shrine}} *[[平将門]] *[[筑土八幡神社]]…戦前まで隣に並んでいた[[八幡宮]]。現在でも筑土八幡町にある。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ 築土神社/平将門を祀る江戸の古社] * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/2006/ 築土祭～よみがえる江戸の情熱（はな）～] * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://edo-chiyoda.jp/chiyoda-yukei_minzoku.html|title=有形民俗文化財|website=千代田区の文化財|publisher= 千代田区|accessdate=2020-6-15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/|title=築土神社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-6-15|ref=harv}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つくとしんしや}} [[Category:千代田区の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:九段]] [[Category:平将門]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = [[:en:September 15|September 15]] }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = [[File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Tsukudo Shrine]] | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = [[:en:September 15|September 15]] }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[World War II]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

}}

The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603463]] Wikidata name: Tsukudo Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.696111|N|139.75|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = [[:en:September 15|September 15]] | location = {{ill|Kudankita|WD=Q3346578}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|qq=|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603463]] Wikidata name: Tsukudo Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.696111|N|139.75|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = [[:en:September 15|September 15]] | location = {{ill|Kudankita|WD=Q3346578}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603463]] Wikidata name: Tsukudo Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.696111|N|139.75|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = [[:en:September 15|September 15]] | location = {{ill|Kudankita|WD=Q3346578}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda]], Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]
