== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]]. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]] in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603463]] Wikidata name: Tsukudo Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.696111|N|139.75|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} | location = {{ill|Kudankita|WD=Q3346578}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}}. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}} in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>

== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukudo Shrine]][[ja:築土神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsukudo Shrine | image = File:Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (02) IMG 4913-4 20180519.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.696111|N|139.75|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1-14-21 Kudan-kita, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} | established = 940 CE | festivals = {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} | location = {{ill|Kudankita|WD=Q3346578}} }}
'''Tsukudo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|築土神社|つくどじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kudan|ja|九段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudan|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}, {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda}}, Tokyo, Japan. Also referred to as '''Tsukudo Myōjin''', it is closely associated with {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, a legendary figure known for his rebellion and subsequent deification.
The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}}. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}} in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}} in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]

The shrine is renowned for its ''Kachimamori'' (Victory Talismans), which are popular among worshippers during New Year's celebrations.
== Enshrined Deities == Tsukudo Shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火邇々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Amatsu Hikohono Ninigi no Mikoto) – the main deity. * {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a revered samurai and deified figure. * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} – the god of learning and scholarship.
During the Edo period, it was believed that the shrine housed relics such as Masakado's skull, portrait, and seated wooden figure. These were lost in the fires of the Tokyo air raids during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, leaving only historical photographs and records.
== History == The origins of Tsukudo Shrine trace back to {{nihongo|940 CE|天慶3rd year }}, when it was established in Tsukudo Village (present-day Otemachi) to enshrine the spirit of Taira no Masakado. Over the centuries, the shrine has been relocated multiple times: * '''1580''': Moved to Kudan Hill (current Kudanzaka area) by {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}}. * '''1616''': Relocated to Tsukudo Hachiman Shrine in modern-day Shinjuku due to the expansion of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}. * '''1874''': Renamed "Tsukudo Shrine" and enshrined Ninigi as the primary deity. * '''1945''': Destroyed during the Tokyo air raids and later rebuilt in 1954 at its current location. * '''1994''': Modernization of the shrine with a reinforced concrete structure.
== festivals == The shrine's primary festival is the {{nihongo|Tsukudo festival|築土祭}}, which culminates in a grand ceremony on September 15. Celebrations often include traditional mikoshi processions, with notable events such as the mikoshi entering {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}} in 2006 for the first time in 147 years.
== Cultural Significance == Tsukudo Shrine is recognized for its historical and cultural contributions: - The shrine's ''komainu'' (guardian lion-dog statues) and ''chikaraishi'' (power stones) are registered cultural properties of Chiyoda Ward.
== Access == The shrine is conveniently located near: * '''Kudanshita Station''' on the Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines.
== Gallery == <gallery> Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (01) IMG 4901-1 20180519.jpg|Pathway to the shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (03) IMG 4910-3 20180519.jpg|Lion-dog statue (dated to 1780) Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (04) IMG 4907-2 20180519.jpg|Adjacent Yotsugi Inari Shrine Tsukudo Jinja Shrine (05) IMG 4915-5 20180519.jpg|View from Kudanzaka towards the main hall </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsukudo.jp/ Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/chiyoda/5542/ Tokyo Shrine Association page]
